zoom-face.name: Classic Q&A PIP Card
zoom-face.psychology: Direct, conversational Q&A. Use when the tone is authoritative/confident and direct. Doctor fills the screen before the card zooms to the corner.
zoom-face.visual: A glassmorphic question card in the center with a countdown progress circle, which zooms down to the top-left corner at the answer frame.

stat-counter.name: Large Statistic Counter
stat-counter.psychology: Use ONLY when the hookText contains a specific statistic, percentage, or ratio (e.g., '90%', '1 in 4'). Must include 'hookStat' field.
stat-counter.visual: A massive number counts up rapidly on the screen, accompanied by an explanatory sub-tagline.

text-slam.name: Bold Text Slam
text-slam.psychology: Use for urgent, warning, or myth-busting tones. High kinetic energy, forces quick attention to the core statement.
text-slam.visual: Words slam onto the screen sequentially with high speed and large typography.

typewriter-bold.name: Bold Classic Typewriter
typewriter-bold.psychology: Use for educational or storytelling tone. Calm, professional, and structured cadence.
typewriter-bold.visual: Large, bold text typed out line-by-line with a blinking vertical cursor.

split-reveal.name: Split Screen Reveal
split-reveal.psychology: Use for dramatic revelations or before/after content. Creates visual contrast.
split-reveal.visual: The screen splits horizontally/vertically, and the doctor's portrait slides up as the text reveals.

word-cascade.name: Word Cascade Fallback
word-cascade.psychology: Use as a general fallback if no other specialized tone fits.
word-cascade.visual: Words cascade down the screen sequentially with elastic spring bounces.

ehr-file.name: Clinical Analytics Telemetry
ehr-file.psychology: Use for high-stakes diagnostic or warning content. Simulates a clinical telemetry brief.
ehr-file.visual: A dark dashboard with patient ID scrambling telemetry, a status critical warning flasher, and a large bottom text slam.

parallax-data.name: Cinematic Parallax Data
parallax-data.psychology: Use to highlight guidlines or system friction in private practices. Creates high-end clinical documentary feel.
parallax-data.visual: Slow panning, desaturated background doctor portrait with an active clinical line wave vector at the bottom.

redacted.name: Confidential Redacted Document
redacted.psychology: Use for conspiracy, corporate/insurance secrets, or controversial data. Spikes immediate curiosity.
redacted.visual: A cream paper document overlaying a desaturated doctor photo. Black block boxes cover key words and shake the screen when unmasked.

typewriter-terminal.name: Terminal Monospace Typewriter
typewriter-terminal.psychology: Use for retro-technical, hacking, or clinical-log style warnings.
typewriter-terminal.visual: Green monospace characters typing out with a blinking neon block cursor, scan lines, and grid overlay.

typewriter-pop.name: Kinetic Word Pop
typewriter-pop.psychology: Use to emphasize each word in a bold statement. Highly engaging and rhythmic.
typewriter-pop.visual: Words spring-scale up one by one. The active word glows in the accent color before settling into white.

typewriter-slide.name: Staggered Mask Slide
typewriter-slide.psychology: Use for sleek, high-end Apple-style product or concept introductions.
typewriter-slide.visual: Words slide up from a clipped baseline mask and fade in with a glowing accent color on the final words.

3d-stack.name: 3D Card Stack Fracture
3d-stack.psychology: Use when debunking a standard belief or industry consensus. ('They told you X, but it's a lie.')
3d-stack.visual: An official card stack fractures along 3D axes and slides away to reveal the doctor's portrait and hook text.

blur-in.name: Kinetic Blur Reveal
blur-in.psychology: Use to build anticipation. Restricts visual information initially, forcing focus on words as they resolve.
blur-in.visual: Text starts highly blurred, dropping into sharp focus line-by-line while the doctor photo unblurs.

parallax-waveform.name: Parallax Voice Waveform
parallax-waveform.psychology: Use when highlighting a voice-centric hook. Immediate proof of voice track activity.
parallax-waveform.visual: Ken Burns zoom on desaturated doctor photo, with an SVG active audio waveform at the footer simulating voice waves.

glitch-cycle.name: Glitch Cycle Alert
glitch-cycle.psychology: Use for systemic errors or alarming contradictions. Simulates technical malfunction.
glitch-cycle.visual: Metric text strobes and glitches rapidly between medical terms before locking on a bold red emergency warning.

mosaic-reframe.name: Mosaic Image Reframe
mosaic-reframe.psychology: Use to represent aggregating multiple clues or research into a single expert conclusion.
mosaic-reframe.visual: Six grid fragments of the doctor's photo slide from off-screen and converge to form the unified photo.

variable-typewriter.name: EHR Variable Typewriter
variable-typewriter.psychology: Use to simulate internal health record entries. Feels like a live clinical diary typing.
variable-typewriter.visual: Text types inside a dark terminal card at human-like variable speeds (pauses on spaces/punctuation), with a circular doctor badge.

3d-carousel.name: 3D Card Carousel Swipe
3d-carousel.psychology: Use when comparing option A, B, and a secret option C. Highlights decision making.
3d-carousel.visual: Three cards rotate in a vertical 3D perspective drum, decelerating and locking onto the target hook card.

matrix-flyby.name: Symptom Matrix Fly-through
matrix-flyby.psychology: Use for complex topics with too much data. Simulates cutting through jargon to find the core answer.
matrix-flyby.visual: Acronyms fly past the camera in 3D space, resolving on a clean presentation of the doctor and hook.

list-countdown.name: Countdown List Knockout
list-countdown.psychology: Use for high density listicles (e.g., '3 main errors'). Extremely organized and structured.
list-countdown.visual: Numbers '01', '02', '03' flash progressively in neon boxes to unmask key bullet points sequentially.

3d-showcase.name: Transform 3D Showcase Switch
3d-showcase.psychology: Use to flip a regulatory or administrative issue into a real-life solution.
3d-showcase.visual: A skewed 3D document flips 180 degrees along the Y-axis to reveal the doctor's photo and answer on the back.
